description: Test cases for the internal-comms skill. Covers writing (3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, general comms), validating drafts, editing, converting between formats, and edge cases. All context is provided inline in the prompt; no external tool access is available in the sandbox. Output is saved as output.md.

test_cases.name: 3P update — Inference team weekly
test_cases.prompt:
Write a 3P update (Progress / Plans / Problems) for the Inference Platform team for the week ending 2026-05-08. Keep each section to 3-5 tight bullets, no fluff. Audience: VP of Engineering and product leadership.

Context this week:
- Shipped latency P99 reduction from 480 ms to 310 ms on claude-sonnet-4-6 via speculative decoding v2
- Completed A/B test showing 12% throughput uplift for batch workloads
- Merged 3 PRs for the new token-streaming endpoint; in staging
- Finished oncall rotation review; updated runbook with 4 new runbook entries

Plans next week:
- Graduate token-streaming endpoint to 10% production traffic
- Begin capacity planning for Q3 GPU cluster expansion
- Resolve 2 open P1 tickets before Thursday

Problems / blockers:
- KV cache memory leak under high concurrency — root cause identified, fix in review
- Dependent on Storage team approval for new S3 bucket policy; unblocked on their side but waiting for sign-off

Save the output to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A 3P update in markdown with three clearly labeled sections: Progress, Plans, Problems. Each section has 3-5 concise bullet points. Total length is under 250 words. Tone is direct, data-driven, no filler. Bullets lead with the outcome or action, not the process.

test_cases.name: 3P update — very sparse context
test_cases.prompt:
Write a 3P update for the Developer Experience team for this week.

Progress: Worked on onboarding docs. Fixed a CLI bug.
Plans: More docs work. Team offsite prep.
Problems: None right now.

Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A 3P update that accurately reflects the sparse information given. Does NOT invent specifics not in the prompt (no made-up PR numbers, no fabricated metrics). Bullets are short and honest about the limited detail. May note that more detail would strengthen the update. Output is under 150 words.

test_cases.name: Company newsletter — May 2026 highlights
test_cases.prompt:
Write the May 2026 internal company newsletter for Anthropic. Audience is all ~1,000 employees. Target length: 20-25 bullets, grouped by theme. Include a warm opening sentence and a closing sentence. Use markdown with section headers. Links should be written as [Link text](URL) using the URLs I provide.

Highlights to include:
- Claude 4 Opus launched to general availability on 2026-05-01 (https://anthropic.com/news/claude-4-opus)
- Company hit 10M API developers milestone (https://intranet.anthropic.com/milestones/10m-devs)
- Safety team published the Model Welfare Policy v2 (https://intranet.anthropic.com/policies/model-welfare-v2)
- Q1 2026 financial results: ARR grew 140% YoY (https://intranet.anthropic.com/finance/q1-2026)
- Engineering all-hands recording posted (https://intranet.anthropic.com/events/allhands-may2026)
- New SF office floor opened — desk booking live (https://intranet.anthropic.com/sf-office/floor-7)
- 3 promotions to Principal Engineer: Ana Lima, Jordan Park, Sam Osei (https://intranet.anthropic.com/people/promotions-may2026)
- Onboarded 42 new hires in April — welcome post (https://intranet.anthropic.com/people/april-welcome)
- Benefits open enrollment closes May 15 (https://intranet.anthropic.com/hr/benefits-2026)
- Claude for Work shipped to enterprise customers (https://intranet.anthropic.com/product/claude-for-work)
- Hackathon winners announced: Team Cascade, Team Lumen, Team Meridian (https://intranet.anthropic.com/events/hackathon-winners)

Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A well-structured newsletter in markdown. Has a brief warm opener (1-2 sentences). Body is 20-25 bullets grouped under 4-6 thematic section headers (e.g., Product, People, Operations, Finance). Every highlight from the prompt appears, and every URL is included as a clickable markdown link. Closes with a brief, upbeat sentence. Tone is energetic, inclusive, and concise. No bullet exceeds 2 lines.

test_cases.name: Company newsletter — minimal highlights
test_cases.prompt:
Write an internal Anthropic newsletter for the week of 2026-04-28. This is a slow news week — only a few items.

Highlights:
- Engineering blog post published: 'How we reduced P99 latency by 35%' (https://anthropic.com/engineering/latency)
- New hire orientation this Thursday at 2pm PT (https://intranet.anthropic.com/events/orientation-april)
- Reminder: performance review self-assessments due April 30 (https://intranet.anthropic.com/hr/perf-review)

Target 8-12 bullets. You can expand each highlight into 1-2 related bullets to reach the count, but do not invent events. Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A newsletter in markdown with 8-12 bullets. Each provided highlight appears and is linked. Expansion bullets are logically derived from the given items (e.g., a reminder about deadlines). No fabricated events. Tone is light and professional. Includes a brief opening and closing line.

test_cases.name: FAQ — return-to-office policy
test_cases.prompt:
Write an internal FAQ document answering common employee questions about Anthropic's updated return-to-office policy effective June 1, 2026. Format as Q&A in markdown. Keep each answer to 2-4 sentences max.

Questions employees are asking:
1. How many days per week are we expected to be in office?
2. Which roles are exempt from the in-office requirement?
3. What if I live more than 50 miles from the nearest office?
4. Can I still work fully remotely if my manager approves it?
5. What happens if I don't comply — is this tied to performance reviews?
6. When does the new policy start and where can I read the full policy?

Policy details to use in your answers:
- Expectation: 3 days/week in-office for most roles (Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday preferred)
- Exempt roles: data center operations, field sales, roles in cities with no Anthropic office
- Remote employees >50 miles away: expected to travel to SF or NYC office 2x per quarter for team weeks
- Manager discretion: exceptions require VP approval, not just manager approval
- Non-compliance: addressed through normal performance management process; not a separate disciplinary track
- Policy effective June 1, 2026; full policy at https://intranet.anthropic.com/hr/rto-policy-2026

Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A markdown FAQ with 6 clearly labeled Q&A pairs. Each answer is 2-4 sentences and uses the policy details provided. Tone is clear, empathetic, and non-threatening. No invented policy details. Includes a link to the full policy doc in the last answer.

test_cases.name: Incident report — API outage
test_cases.prompt:
Write an internal incident report for a partial API outage that affected Anthropic's Claude API on 2026-05-06. Audience: Engineering leadership and the SRE team. Use a standard incident report structure.

Details:
- Incident ID: INC-2026-0506
- Severity: P1
- Start: 2026-05-06 14:32 UTC
- End: 2026-05-06 16:18 UTC
- Duration: 1 hour 46 minutes
- Impact: ~22% of API requests failing with HTTP 503; batch workloads most affected; real-time API degraded but not fully down
- Root cause: A misconfigured load balancer rule deployed at 14:28 UTC caused 22% of traffic to route to a drained backend pool
- Detection: Automated alerting fired at 14:35 UTC (3 min after start); on-call engineer paged
- Mitigation: Load balancer config rolled back at 15:50 UTC; full traffic recovery confirmed by 16:18 UTC
- Contributing factor: The change was deployed without a staged rollout; config linting did not catch the drained-pool reference
- Action items:
  1. Add drained-pool validation to lb-config linter (owner: Platform Infra, due 2026-05-20)
  2. Require staged rollout for all lb config changes (owner: SRE, due 2026-05-27)
  3. Reduce alert-to-page SLA from 5 min to 2 min (owner: Observability team, due 2026-06-01)

Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A structured incident report in markdown with clearly labeled sections: Summary, Timeline, Root Cause, Impact, Mitigation, Action Items. Each section contains the relevant details from the prompt. Timeline entries are formatted with timestamps. Action items include owner and due date. Tone is factual and professional, no finger-pointing language. Total length 300-500 words.

test_cases.name: Status report — product launch readiness
test_cases.prompt:
Write a product launch readiness status report for Claude for Work, targeting launch on 2026-06-01. Audience: CEO and executive team. Format: markdown, concise.

Status as of 2026-05-08:
- Engineering: GREEN — all P0 features shipped; 4 P1 bugs open, all tracked
- Security review: GREEN — completed 2026-04-30
- Legal / privacy: YELLOW — DPA templates pending review for EU customers; expected sign-off 2026-05-15
- Marketing: GREEN — launch blog post drafted; press embargo set
- Support readiness: YELLOW — help center articles 80% complete; training for support team scheduled May 19-20
- Sales enablement: GREEN — pitch deck and demo environment ready
- Overall status: YELLOW — on track for June 1 if legal/support items close by May 20

Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A launch readiness report in markdown. Has a one-paragraph executive summary at the top. Then a status table or bulleted list showing each workstream with its color status (GREEN/YELLOW/RED) and a brief note. Ends with an 'Overall' status and next steps. Total 200-350 words. Tone is confident but honest about yellows.

test_cases.name: Leadership update — reorg announcement
test_cases.prompt:
Write an internal leadership update announcing a reorganization of the Engineering division, effective 2026-06-01. The email is from CTO Priya Nair. Tone should be transparent, positive about the future, and empathetic about uncertainty. Format: prose email body (no bullet lists). Length: ~250 words.

Reorg details:
- Reason: scaling from 200 to 400 engineers requires clearer ownership and faster decision-making
- Change: Splitting the Engineering org into two VPs: Infrastructure (VP: Marcus Webb) and Product Engineering (VP: Aisha Delgado)
- Both VPs report to Priya
- No layoffs; no team merges; individual reporting lines stay the same for most people
- A small number of people (~15) will see their reporting line change — managers will reach out 1:1 this week
- Q&A town hall: May 14 at 11am PT (https://intranet.anthropic.com/events/eng-townhall-may14)
- Questions: eng-reorg@anthropic.com

Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A prose email body (~250 words, no bullet lists) from CTO Priya Nair. Clearly explains the reason for the reorg, introduces the two new VP roles with names, reassures employees no layoffs are happening, notes that ~15 people will see reporting changes, and directs people to the town hall (with link) and the email address for questions. Tone is warm, direct, and empathetic. Includes a sign-off line.

test_cases.name: Project update — data platform migration
test_cases.prompt:
Write a project update for the Data Platform Migration (BigQuery → internal lakehouse). Audience: Technical program managers and engineering leads. Format: structured markdown with sections.

Project status as of 2026-05-08:
- Project phase: Phase 2 of 3 (data validation and dual-write)
- Schedule: On track; Phase 2 completes May 30
- Phase 3 (cutover) begins June 2; targeted completion July 15
- Key milestone this week: Dual-write validation passed for events schema (128 tables)
- Remaining work in Phase 2: Validate 40 remaining tables in analytics schema; load test at 150% peak throughput
- Risks: Load test result unknown — if throughput SLO is missed, Phase 3 start may slip 2 weeks
- Dependencies: Data Science team must freeze analytics schema by May 22 (confirmed)
- Team: 6 engineers, 1 PM (Chen Li), 1 TPM (Ravi Kapoor)

Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A structured project update in markdown with sections: Overview, Milestone Status, Upcoming Work, Risks & Dependencies, Team. Data from the prompt appears in the correct sections. Includes the key milestone and phase dates. Risk is clearly called out with its potential impact. Tone is informative and precise. Total 200-350 words.

test_cases.name: General comms — office closure notice
test_cases.prompt:
Write an internal communication announcing that the New York office will be closed for renovation from July 1 to July 31, 2026. Audience: all NYC-based employees (~80 people). Format: short prose email body, under 150 words. From: Workplace team.

Details:
- Closure period: July 1–31, 2026
- Reason: HVAC and electrical infrastructure upgrade
- During closure: employees may work from home or use WeWork day passes (details at https://intranet.anthropic.com/nyc-closure-2026)
- Equipment pickup: employees can retrieve personal items and peripherals June 27-30, 9am–5pm
- Building access: key cards will be deactivated July 1; reactivated August 1
- Questions: workplace-nyc@anthropic.com

Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A short prose email body under 150 words. Covers the closure dates, reason, work-from-home/WeWork option (with link), equipment pickup window, and contact email. Tone is helpful and practical. No bullet points — prose paragraphs only.

test_cases.name: Validate 3P update — missing Problems section
test_cases.prompt:
Review this 3P update draft and identify any format or content issues. Output your findings as a bulleted list in output.md.

Draft:
---
**Progress**
- Shipped new dashboard to 100% of users
- Resolved 12 support tickets
- Completed design review for v2 feature

**Plans**
- Start building v2 feature next week
- Meet with PM to align on roadmap
---

Expected format: 3P update = three sections (Progress, Plans, Problems), each with 3-5 bullets. Audience is VP-level. Each bullet should lead with the outcome or action, not process.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A bulleted list identifying format issues in the draft. Must flag: missing Problems section, too few bullets in Plans (only 2), and vague bullets (e.g., 'Completed design review' doesn't state outcome). May note missing metrics in Progress. Does NOT rewrite the update — only lists issues. 150-300 words.

test_cases.name: Validate newsletter — missing links
test_cases.prompt:
Review this internal newsletter draft for issues. Output your findings as a bulleted list in output.md.

Draft:
---
## Product
- Claude 4 Opus is now generally available. Check it out!
- Claude for Work shipped to enterprise customers last week.

## People
- We welcomed 35 new hires in April.
- Three engineers were promoted to Principal level: Ana, Jordan, and Sam.

## Operations
- Open enrollment for benefits closes May 15. Don't forget!
---

Expected format: company newsletter with 20-25 bullets, section headers, markdown links on every event/announcement, brief opener and closer, max 2 lines per bullet.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A bulleted list of issues identifying: far too few bullets (only 6 vs 20-25 target), zero markdown links on any item, no opener or closer, bullets are vague (no specifics, no links). May note the Claude 4 Opus bullet lacks a link and specifics. Does NOT rewrite the newsletter — only lists issues. 150-250 words.

test_cases.name: Validate FAQ — answers too long
test_cases.prompt:
Review this FAQ draft and identify issues. Output findings in output.md.

Draft:
---
**Q: When does open enrollment close?**
A: Open enrollment closes on May 22, 2026. This is an important deadline and you should make sure you complete your benefits elections before this date. Failure to do so will result in you being automatically enrolled in the default plan, which may not be the best option for you. You should log in to the benefits portal and review all available options including medical, dental, vision, FSA, HSA, and the new pet insurance option before the deadline. It is strongly recommended that you consult with HR if you have questions.

**Q: Do I have to re-enroll if I want to keep the same plan?**
A: If you want to keep the same plan you currently have, you do not need to actively make any elections. You will be passively re-enrolled in your current medical, dental, and vision plans. However, FSA and HSA accounts require active re-enrollment every year regardless of what you had previously, so be sure to log in and re-enroll if you wish to continue with those accounts.
---

Expected format: FAQ answers should be 2-4 sentences max, direct, no filler.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A bulleted list identifying that both answers are too long (should be 2-4 sentences, not 5-8). First answer has filler and redundancy. Second answer is better but still slightly long. Notes that the 2-4 sentence target is not met. Does NOT rewrite the FAQ. 100-200 words.

test_cases.name: Validate incident report — missing action items
test_cases.prompt:
Review this incident report draft and list all issues. Output to output.md.

Draft:
---
## Incident Summary
**ID:** INC-2026-0412  
**Severity:** P2  
**Duration:** 45 minutes

## What Happened
A memory leak in the caching layer caused elevated error rates on the /completions endpoint starting at 09:15 UTC. The on-call engineer detected the issue at 09:22 and restarted the affected pods at 09:58 UTC. Error rates returned to baseline by 10:00 UTC.

## Root Cause
A code change deployed at 09:10 introduced an unbounded cache growth pattern that was not caught in staging.
---

Expected format: incident report must include: Summary (ID, severity, start/end times, duration, impact), Timeline with timestamps, Root Cause, Action Items with owners and due dates.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A bulleted list identifying missing sections: no Timeline with timestamps, no Impact quantified, no Action Items with owners/due dates, start and end timestamps not given (only duration). Notes that the root cause is stated but lacks detail about why staging didn't catch it. Does NOT rewrite the report. 150-250 words.

test_cases.name: Edit 3P update — sharpen bullets
test_cases.prompt:
Edit this 3P update to make the bullets more outcome-focused and concise. Remove any filler. Do not add information not in the original. Save the improved version to output.md.

Original:
---
**Progress**
- We spent time this week working on the new API caching layer and made some good progress on it
- The team had a meeting to discuss the roadmap and we aligned on priorities
- Fixed a bug that was causing occasional timeouts for some users in the EU region

**Plans**
- We are planning to continue working on the caching layer next week and hopefully finish it
- There will be a design review on Thursday for the next feature

**Problems**
- We are experiencing some slowness in getting reviews on our PRs because the team is busy
---
test_cases.expected_behavior: An edited 3P update where bullets are rewritten to lead with outcomes (e.g., 'API caching layer: initial implementation complete' not 'We spent time working on...'). Filler phrases like 'We spent time', 'made some good progress', 'hopefully' are removed. No new facts are added. Format preserved (3 sections, bullets). Under 200 words.

test_cases.name: Edit newsletter — add structure and links
test_cases.prompt:
Edit this newsletter to add section headers, ensure all items have markdown links, and expand to 15-18 bullets total. Do not invent events; you may expand existing items into 1-2 related bullets. Save to output.md.

Original (no section headers, no links):
---
- Claude 4 Opus launched to GA. Link: https://anthropic.com/news/claude-4-opus
- Company hit 10M API developers milestone. Link: https://intranet.anthropic.com/milestones/10m-devs
- Q1 2026 ARR grew 140% YoY. Link: https://intranet.anthropic.com/finance/q1-2026
- 42 new hires in April. Link: https://intranet.anthropic.com/people/april-welcome
- Benefits open enrollment closes May 15. Link: https://intranet.anthropic.com/hr/benefits-2026
---
test_cases.expected_behavior: An edited newsletter with 4-5 section headers grouping related items. All links are formatted as markdown [text](url). Total 15-18 bullets. Any expansion bullets are logically related to an existing item (e.g., expanding the 10M devs milestone into 'what this means for the community'). No invented events. Has a brief opener and closer.

test_cases.name: Edit FAQ — shorten long answers
test_cases.prompt:
Edit this FAQ to reduce every answer to 2-4 sentences maximum. Preserve all key facts. Save to output.md.

Original:
---
**Q: How many days per week do we need to be in office?**
A: The updated return-to-office policy requires employees in eligible roles to be in the office for a minimum of three days per week. The preferred days are Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday, though you have flexibility on which days you choose as long as you meet the three-day minimum for the week. It is important to coordinate with your team so that there is overlap, especially for collaborative work and meetings that are better done in person. Some teams may have different expectations set by their leadership, so please also check with your manager.

**Q: What if I live more than 50 miles from the office?**
A: If you live more than 50 miles from the nearest Anthropic office, you are considered a remote employee and the standard three-day-per-week in-office expectation does not apply to you. Instead, you will be expected to travel to either the San Francisco or New York City office twice per quarter for team weeks, which are coordinated by your team lead. Travel costs for these team week visits are covered by the company in accordance with the standard travel expense policy. Please reach out to your manager or People Ops if you have questions about how this applies to your specific situation.
---
test_cases.expected_behavior: An edited FAQ where both answers are reduced to 2-4 sentences each. All key facts are preserved: 3 days/week, Tue/Wed/Thu preferred, 50-mile threshold, 2x per quarter for team weeks, travel covered. No new facts added. Filler sentences removed. Format preserved.

test_cases.name: Edit incident report — add missing action items
test_cases.prompt:
The following incident report is missing an Action Items section. Add it based on the information in the report. Do not change any existing content. Save the updated report to output.md.

Report:
---
## Incident Summary
**ID:** INC-2026-0506  
**Severity:** P1  
**Start:** 2026-05-06 14:32 UTC  
**End:** 2026-05-06 16:18 UTC  
**Impact:** ~22% of API requests failing with HTTP 503; batch workloads most affected

## Root Cause
A misconfigured load balancer rule was deployed without staged rollout. The config linter did not catch a drained-pool reference.

## Timeline
- 14:28 UTC: Load balancer config deployed
- 14:32 UTC: Error rate spike detected by monitoring
- 14:35 UTC: Automated alert fired; on-call paged
- 15:50 UTC: Config rolled back
- 16:18 UTC: Full recovery confirmed
---

Base the action items on the root cause: (1) fix the linter to catch drained-pool references, (2) require staged rollouts for lb config changes, (3) reduce alert-to-page latency.
test_cases.expected_behavior: The original report is reproduced exactly, with an 'Action Items' section appended. The section contains 3 action items derived from the root cause, each with a brief description, an owner placeholder (e.g., 'Owner: TBD' or a reasonable team name like 'Platform Infra'), and a suggested due date. No other content is changed.

test_cases.name: Convert 3P update to newsletter bullets
test_cases.prompt:
Convert this 3P update into 3-5 newsletter bullets suitable for the company-wide internal newsletter. Newsletter bullets should be concise, celebrate wins, and link to relevant resources where naturally appropriate. Translate internal jargon where needed. Save to output.md.

3P Update (Inference Platform, week of 2026-05-05):
---
**Progress**
- Shipped speculative decoding v2; P99 latency down from 480 ms to 310 ms on claude-sonnet-4-6
- A/B test complete: 12% throughput uplift for batch workloads confirmed
- Token-streaming endpoint merged; in staging

**Plans**
- Graduate token-streaming to 10% production traffic
- Begin Q3 GPU capacity planning

**Problems**
- KV cache memory leak identified; fix in review
---
test_cases.expected_behavior: 3-5 newsletter-style markdown bullets that highlight the Progress wins (latency reduction with the numbers, throughput uplift). Problems and Plans are not included unless they are newsworthy (KV cache leak should be omitted). Tone is celebratory and accessible to a non-engineer audience. No jargon unexplained. Bullets are 1-2 lines each.

test_cases.name: Convert FAQ to a prose paragraph
test_cases.prompt:
Convert this FAQ into a single prose paragraph suitable for inclusion in an all-hands email. The paragraph should cover all the same information but read as flowing prose, not Q&A. Target 80-120 words. Save to output.md.

FAQ:
---
**Q: When does open enrollment close?**
A: Open enrollment closes May 22, 2026. Log in at https://benefits.anthropic.com.

**Q: Do I need to re-enroll to keep my current plan?**
A: For medical, dental, and vision, no — you'll be passively re-enrolled. FSA and HSA require active re-enrollment every year.

**Q: Who do I contact with questions?**
A: Email benefits@anthropic.com or visit #people-benefits on Slack.
---
test_cases.expected_behavior: A single prose paragraph of 80-120 words that naturally incorporates: the May 22 deadline, the benefits portal URL, the passive enrollment rule (with the FSA/HSA exception), and the contact info. No Q&A format. No bullet points. Reads as a natural paragraph in an email context.

test_cases.name: Edge — missing team name
test_cases.prompt:
Write a 3P update for this week.

Progress: Shipped the new feature. Fixed some bugs. Had a retrospective.
Plans: Keep working on the roadmap. Finish the remaining tickets.
Problems: Nothing major.

Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A 3P update that uses the information given without inventing a team name. Uses a placeholder like '[Team Name]' or notes that the team name was not provided. Does NOT fabricate team names, metrics, or specific feature names not in the prompt. Bullets accurately reflect the sparse input. Output is under 150 words.

test_cases.name: Edge — conflicting format requirements
test_cases.prompt:
Write a 3P update for the Data team. Also make it a newsletter with 20 bullets and a warm opener. Save to output.md.

Context:
- Finished migrating 50 tables to the new lakehouse
- Starting load testing next week
- Blocked on schema freeze approval from the Analytics team
test_cases.expected_behavior: The output resolves the conflicting requirements. Preferred approach: produces a 3P update (the dominant, more specific format) and notes that the newsletter format was not compatible with a 3P update — or asks for clarification in a comment. Does NOT try to awkwardly merge both formats. If it picks one, it explains why at the top. Under 200 words.

test_cases.name: Edge — non-English input, English output requested
test_cases.prompt:
Écrire une mise à jour 3P pour l'équipe Sécurité pour cette semaine. Output in English please.

Contexte:
- Déploiement du classificateur v3.1 terminé
- Test d'intrusion réalisé sur le nouveau module multimodal
- Problème : revue légale du document de politique encore en attente

Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A 3P update written entirely in English, accurately translating the French context: classifier v3.1 deployed, penetration testing completed on new multimodal module, legal review of policy document still pending. Uses 'Security team' as the team name (translated). Correct 3P format with 3 sections, concise bullets. Total under 200 words.

test_cases.name: Convert incident report to exec one-pager
test_cases.prompt:
Convert this incident report into a one-paragraph executive summary for VP-and-above leadership readout. Target 80-120 words (no bullets, no section headers). Cover: what failed, customer impact, root cause in one sentence, current status, top action item. Save to output.md.

Incident report:
---
## Incident Summary
**ID:** INC-2026-0506  
**Severity:** P1  
**Start:** 2026-05-06 14:32 UTC  
**End:** 2026-05-06 15:48 UTC  
**Duration:** 76 minutes

## Timeline
- 14:32 UTC: Error rate on /v1/messages spikes from 0.1% to 32%
- 14:38 UTC: PagerDuty alert; on-call investigates
- 14:55 UTC: Root cause identified (memory leak in router v2.3.1)
- 15:10 UTC: Hotfix deployed to canary; error rate dropping
- 15:48 UTC: Full rollout complete; error rate <0.1%

## Impact
- ~12,000 paying customer requests affected
- Slack auto-credits issued

## Root Cause
Router v2.3.1 leaked HTTP connections on stream timeout, exhausting the pool after ~90 min of normal traffic.

## Action Items
- Roll forward to router v2.3.2 with connection lifecycle test (@infra-team, due 2026-05-13)
- Add memory-leak load test to release gate (@qa-team, due 2026-05-20)
test_cases.expected_behavior: A single paragraph of 80-120 words (no bullets, no headers) covering: P1 /v1/messages error spike (32%), 76-minute duration, ~12k requests affected, root cause (router v2.3.1 connection leak), status (resolved via hotfix to v2.3.2 rollout), and the top action item (release-gate memory-leak test). Tone is executive-direct, no jargon. No invented facts.

test_cases.name: Convert newsletter highlights to Slack announcement
test_cases.prompt:
Convert these three newsletter highlights into a single Slack announcement post for the #announcements channel. Casual but professional tone, 60-100 words, use 1-2 emoji naturally, keep any provided links. Save to output.md.

Highlights:
- We hit 100M API calls in April — a 3x YoY milestone.
- Claude 4 Opus is now generally available on the API. [Release notes](https://anthropic.com/news/c4-opus-ga)
- New York office opens for tours June 15. RSVP by June 10: [link](https://intranet.anthropic.com/ny-tour)
test_cases.expected_behavior: A Slack announcement post (60-100 words). Covers all three highlights with their key facts (100M April calls / 3x YoY, Opus GA, NY office tour June 15 with June 10 RSVP). Markdown links preserved. Tone is casual-professional. 1-2 emoji used naturally (not stacked). No bullets — flows as a single post.

test_cases.name: Edge — bare prompt with no context
test_cases.prompt: Write a 3P update for the platform team. Save to output.md.
test_cases.expected_behavior: The output does NOT invent specific metrics, project names, dates, or blockers. Acceptable behaviors: (a) a 3P scaffold using placeholders like '[shipped: ...]' and noting that context was not provided; (b) a one-line note explaining that more information is needed before a useful 3P can be written. What MUST NOT happen: fabricated metrics or made-up project names presented as facts.

test_cases.name: Edge — user requests bullets in 3P (conflicts with rule)
test_cases.prompt:
Write a 3P update for the SRE team this week using bullet points throughout — each Progress, Plans, Problems section should be a bulleted list. Save to output.md.

Context:
- Migrated 30% of services off the legacy load balancer
- Reduced on-call paging volume by 22% with smarter alert grouping
- Need approval to retire the v1 metrics pipeline next sprint
test_cases.expected_behavior: The output respects the 3P format rule (sentences, not bullets) — the SKILL.md format spec for 3P is 'Never use bullet points — only sentences'. Acceptable: produce a sentences-based 3P AND include a one-line note explaining the format constraint. NOT acceptable: silently follow the user request and emit bulleted 3P, violating the format rule.
